channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - RAG & Retrieval
channel.description: Latest RAG & Retrieval news in Context Engineering

item.title: Condition-Gated Reasoning for Context-Dependent Biomedical Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2602.17911v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current biomedical question answering (QA) systems often assume that medical knowledge applies uniformly, yet real-world clinical reasoning is inherently conditional: nearly every decision depends on patient-specific factors such as comorbidities and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, framework, reasoning, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CUICurate: A GraphRAG-based Framework for Automated Clinical Concept Curation for NLP applications
item.description: arXiv:2602.17949v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background: Clinical named entity recognition tools commonly map free text to Unified Medical Language System (UMLS) Concept Unique Identifiers (CUIs). For many downstream tasks, however, the clinically meaningful unit is not a single CUI but a concep...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, retrieval, GPT, framework, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Decomposing Retrieval Failures in RAG for Long-Document Financial Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2602.17981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation is increasingly used for financial question answering over long regulatory filings, yet reliability depends on retrieving the exact context needed to justify answers in high stakes settings. We study a frequent failure m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, analysis, RAG, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Ontology-Guided Neuro-Symbolic Inference: Grounding Language Models with Mathematical Domain Knowledge
item.description: arXiv:2602.17826v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models exhibit fundamental limitations -- hallucination, brittleness, and lack of formal grounding -- that are particularly problematic in high-stakes specialist fields requiring verifiable reasoning. I investigate whether formal domain ontol...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, prompt, reasoning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: airweave - Open-source context retrieval layer for AI agents
item.description: Open-source context retrieval layer for AI agents<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, augmented, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, retrieval, framework, reasoning, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: graphrag - A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.
item.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, API, RAG, augmented, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: Diffusing to Coordinate: Efficient Online Multi-Agent Diffusion Policies
item.description: arXiv:2602.18291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Online Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) is a prominent framework for efficient agent coordination. Crucially, enhancing policy expressiveness is pivotal for achieving superior performance. Diffusion-based generative models are well-positioned...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, image, RAG, augmented, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SOMtime the World Ain$'$t Fair: Violating Fairness Using Self-Organizing Maps
item.description: arXiv:2602.18201v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Unsupervised representations are widely assumed to be neutral with respect to sensitive attributes when those attributes are withheld from training. We show that this assumption is false. Using SOMtime, a topology-preserving representation method base...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
